headline: Data Center Cost Shift to Large Users
short_summary: This bill requires Virginia's principal electric utility to seek approval for new rate structures that allocate the costs of expanding generation capacity to customers with high demand, primarily data centers, aiming to save residential electricity customers money.
status: Last Action: Signed by President on 2026-04-22
supporters: Residential electricity customers/ratepayers, Del. Destiny LeVere Bolling, Sen. Louise Lucas, Dominion Energy (initially), customer advocates.
opponents: Data centers, Data Center Coalition, Governor Abigail Spanberger (regarding amendments).
news_headline: Virginia governor amends bills that shift costs onto data centers. Critics say her tweaks weaken them.
news_summary: Governor Abigail Spanberger signed and suggested changes to HB 1393 and SB 253, which aim to shift more energy costs onto data centers to benefit residential customers and expand a power line burial program. Critics argued the governor's amendments weakened the original intent of the bills to save residential electricity customers money.

headline: Data Center Water Use Transparency Mandated
short_summary: This bill requires developers of proposed industrial facilities, including data centers, to provide annual water consumption projections as part of rezoning and special use permit applications, and water users providing water to data centers to report total water volume to the State Water Control Board.
status: Last Action: Acts of Assembly Chapter text (CHAP0896) on 2026-04-13
supporters: Local communities/residents, environmental advocates, lawmakers seeking transparency, Sen. Kannan Srinivasan, Sen. Dave Marsden.
opponents: Data center industry (implied, due to increased reporting burden).
news_headline: States push to end secrecy over data center water use
news_summary: Lawmakers in several states, including Virginia, are introducing legislation to require data centers to report their water use, driven by concerns about the environmental footprint of the AI boom. This effort aims to increase transparency and manage finite water resources, drawing bipartisan support and some skepticism.

headline: New Data Center Siting and Noise Regulations
short_summary: This bill establishes a new permit process for high energy use facilities, including data centers, requiring site assessments to evaluate noise impacts on residences and schools, and allowing localities to require analysis of effects on water, agricultural land, and historic sites.
status: Last Action: Incorporated by Counties, Cities and Towns (HB153-Thomas) (Voice Vote) on 2026-01-23
supporters: Local communities/residents, Del. Josh Thomas, Southern Environmental Law Center (SELC), Chesapeake Climate Action Network.
opponents: Data center developers, Governor Glenn Youngkin (vetoed similar bills in 2025).
news_headline: These bills aim to regulate Virginia data center siting, generator use and SCC oversight
news_summary: HB 153, sponsored by Del. Josh Thomas, allows localities to require site assessments for high energy users applying for permits, addressing concerns about data center locations, sound profiles, and environmental impacts. This comes as Virginia grapples with an energy crisis and growing local opposition to data center proliferation.
